# Build Package — POC
# Generated by Architecture Agent → Decomposition Agent
# 3 components · 1 waves · 3× parallelism · 67% time savings

---
## Part 1: Dense Architecture Specification

# Dense Architecture Specification
# Density Score: 0.507

## Purpose
# Resolution: 67.5%

- Hello World weather app for developers learning API integration
- developers learning API integration
- search city, see temp/humidity/wind + 5-day forecast
- city autocomplete, current conditions, forecast cards

## Data Model
# Resolution: 64.0%

- City (name NOT NULL, country_code), WeatherData (city_id FK, temp, humidity, wind_speed, fetched_at), Forecast (city_id FK, date, high, low, description)
- City has-many WeatherData, City has-many Forecast
- City.name NOT NULL
- Index on City.name

## API
# Resolution: 48.0%

- GET /api/weather?city={name}, GET /api/forecast?city={name}&days=5, GET /api/cities?q={query}
- JSON responses
- No WebSocket

## Tech Stack
# Resolution: 68.0%

- Python 3.12
- Flask 3.0
- SQLite
- In-memory cache 5min TTL

## Auth
# Resolution: 40.0%

- API key auth via X-API-Key header
- single tier, no roles

## Deployment
# Resolution: 40.0%

- Docker container
- GitHub Actions CI

## Error Handling
# Resolution: 40.0%

- Error codes 400/401/404/500/502
- Retry upstream OpenWeatherMap 3x exponential backoff

## Performance
# Resolution: 20.0%

- P95 under 200ms cached

## Security
# Resolution: 60.0%

- Input sanitization max 100 chars
- CORS allow-all
- Rate limit 60/min per key

## Testing
# Resolution: 60.0%

- pytest
- 70% coverage
- mock weather API

## All Constraints (Flat)

- Hello World weather app for developers learning API integration
- developers learning API integration
- search city, see temp/humidity/wind + 5-day forecast
- city autocomplete, current conditions, forecast cards
- City (name NOT NULL, country_code), WeatherData (city_id FK, temp, humidity, wind_speed, fetched_at), Forecast (city_id FK, date, high, low, description)
- City has-many WeatherData, City has-many Forecast
- City.name NOT NULL
- Index on City.name
- GET /api/weather?city={name}, GET /api/forecast?city={name}&days=5, GET /api/cities?q={query}
- JSON responses
- No WebSocket
- Python 3.12
- Flask 3.0
- SQLite
- In-memory cache 5min TTL
- API key auth via X-API-Key header
- single tier, no roles
- Docker container
- GitHub Actions CI
- Error codes 400/401/404/500/502
- Retry upstream OpenWeatherMap 3x exponential backoff
- P95 under 200ms cached
- Input sanitization max 100 chars
- CORS allow-all
- Rate limit 60/min per key
- pytest
- 70% coverage
- mock weather API

## Implementation Rules

- Every architectural decision MUST trace to a constraint above
- If a dimension has no constraint, ASK — do not invent
- Prefer explicit over implicit in all generated code
- No defaults: every value must come from this spec

---
## Part 2: Wave Plan

# Wave Plan
# Phase: POC | Components: 3 | Waves: 1 | Parallelism: 3x | Time Savings: 67%

## Wave 0: Foundation (Models + Config)
# Components: 3 | Parallel: 3
- WeatherHandler [api] — REST endpoints for weather resource
- ForecastHandler [api] — REST endpoints for forecast resource
- CityHandler [api] — REST endpoints for city resource

## Interface Contracts
- IAuthService: authenticate(credentials: AuthCredentials) -> AuthToken, validate_token(token: str) -> TokenPayload, authorize(user_id: str, permission: str) -> bool

## Dependency Graph
- auth-middleware -> auth-service (consumes IAuthService)
- weather-handler -> auth-service (consumes IAuthService)
- forecast-handler -> auth-service (consumes IAuthService)
- city-handler -> auth-service (consumes IAuthService)

---
## Part 3: Interface Contracts

Build these interfaces FIRST (Wave 0). All components depend on these type definitions.

### IAuthService
Owner: AuthService
Types: AuthCredentials, AuthToken, TokenPayload
```
  authenticate(credentials: AuthCredentials) -> AuthToken
  validate_token(token: str) -> TokenPayload
  authorize(user_id: str, permission: str) -> bool
```

---
## Part 4: Component Build Prompts

Execute these prompts in wave order. Components within the same wave can be built in parallel.

### Wave 0: Foundation (Models + Config)

#### WeatherHandler

```text
Build a handler component named `WeatherHandler`.
Purpose: REST endpoints for weather resource.

Constraints from the architecture spec:
- GET /api/weather?city={name}, GET /api/forecast?city={name}&days=5, GET /api/cities?q={query}

Depends on (import, do not implement): IAuthService
```

#### ForecastHandler

```text
Build a handler component named `ForecastHandler`.
Purpose: REST endpoints for forecast resource.

Constraints from the architecture spec:
- GET /api/weather?city={name}, GET /api/forecast?city={name}&days=5, GET /api/cities?q={query}

Depends on (import, do not implement): IAuthService
```

#### CityHandler

```text
Build a handler component named `CityHandler`.
Purpose: REST endpoints for city resource.

Constraints from the architecture spec:
- GET /api/weather?city={name}, GET /api/forecast?city={name}&days=5, GET /api/cities?q={query}

Depends on (import, do not implement): IAuthService
```

---
## Part 5: Dependency Graph

```
auth-middleware → auth-service  (consumes IAuthService)
weather-handler → auth-service  (consumes IAuthService)
forecast-handler → auth-service  (consumes IAuthService)
city-handler → auth-service  (consumes IAuthService)
```
